Location: House of Blues Houston, 1204 Caroline Street, Houston, TX

BOX OFFICE:6PM DOORS: 7PM All Ages Welcome We're cashless! All points of sale will only accept credit, debit or mobile pay (such as Apple Pay or Google Pay). Will call tickets can be picked up on day of show at the Box Office. Box Office opens 1 hour prior to door times. You will need a valid photo ID and the credit card used for purchase. ADA tickets can be purchased online - you do not need to call the box office to place an order. Look for the logo on the Find Tickets page to view all available accessible seats. We do have accessible seating in every price range but be advised it may sell out. We have very limited seating available for guests on the day of the event so please make sure you purchase accessible seating when you order tickets. Bags up to 12" x 6" x 12" are allowed in the venue. All bags will be searched prior to entry. item.description:

Location: White Oak Music Hall - Downstairs, 2915 N Main St, Houston, TX

Doors: 6pm This show is 21+ All General Admission Tickets are Standing Room only IMPORTANT: WHITE OAK MUSIC HALL IS A CASHLESS VENUE. Please be sure to bring a debit or credit card. TICKET PACKAGE DESCRIPTIONS: GA Tickets - Doors at 6PM. Your entry into Solistice Sessions -- Full access to floor viewing area, outside patio, music, and bars. Show up and immerse yourself in the energy from the first beat to the last. VIP Tickets - Expedited Entry Line. Beat the crowd, skip the line, and settle into an elevated viewing area with the best sightlines to the stage. Includes access to 2nd floor mezzanine and a dedicated VIP bar all night long. Tables - Everything VIP, elevated. All VIP amenities plus your own hightop or stageside table, a bottle package to start the party right, and discounted bottles once your initial package is done. item.title: Earlybirds Club
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: White Oak Music Hall - Downstairs, 2915 N Main St, Houston, TX

Doors: 6pm Earlybirds Club is a dance party for women and gender expansive folks that starts at 6pm and ends at 10pm ? because we have sh*t to do in the mornin DJ Caribbeats spins the songs that live in your bones: 80s, 90s, and 2000s pop, hip-hop, new wave, and R&B. But Earlybirds Club is more than a night out. It?s a permission slip to take up space. To be loud, weird, wild, sweaty, sparkly, sentimental, and completely yourself. It?s a judgment-free dance floor built on joy, kindness, connection, and the shared understanding that sensible footwear is a love language. And because joy is even better when it gives back, 10% of ticket proceeds go to the Susie Lee Legacy Fund, supporting nonprofits that uplift women, gender-expansive people, and communities who need it most. This show is 21+ All General Admission Tickets are Standing Room only IMPORTANT: WHITE OAK MUSIC HALL IS A CASHLESS VENUE. Please be sure to bring a debit or credit card. Doors: 6pm Earlybirds Club is a dance party for women and gender expansive folks that starts at 6pm and ends at 10pm ? because we have sh*t to do in the mornin DJ Caribbeats spins the songs that live in your bones: 80s, 90s, and 2000s pop, hip-hop, new wave, and R&B. But Earlybirds Club is more than a night out. It?s a permission slip to take up space. To be loud, weird, wild, sweaty, sparkly, sentimental, and completely yourself. It?s a judgment-free dance floor built on joy, kindness, connection, and the shared understanding that sensible footwear is a love language. And because joy is even better when it gives back, 10% of ticket proceeds go to the Susie Lee Legacy Fund, supporting nonprofits that uplift women, gender-expansive people, and communities who need it most. item.title: The Marvelous Miss Gender starring BOSCO - Ages 18+ w/ Valid ID
item.description:
Wednesday, July 22, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: House of Blues Houston, 1204 Caroline Street, Houston, TX

Murray & Peter Present: "The Marvelous Miss Gender" starring BOSCO. From RuPaul's Drag Race Season 14, 2022 and most recently All Stars 10 from 2025, BOSCO is now headlining her first ever solo tour. Meet & Greet add-on tickets are sold separately. To attend the M&G, fans must also purchase a ticket to the event. Show starts at 8pm. There will be a 20-minute intermission. Ages 18+ welcome w/ Valid ID for entry. Check DragFans.com and HereIsBosco.com for tour info VIP Tables seat 4 humans and are first come first serve if you purchase in that section. Please arrive early so that our hosts may seat your party together. Murray & Peter Present: "The Marvelous Miss Gender" starring BOSCO. Will call tickets can be picked up on day of show at the Box Office. Box Office opens 1 hour prior to door times. You will need a valid photo ID and the credit card used for purchase. ADA tickets can be purchased online - you do not need to call the box office to place an order. Look for the logo on the Find Tickets page to view all available accessible seats. We do have accessible seating in every price range but be advised it may sell out. We have very limited seating available for guests on the day of the event so please make sure you purchase accessible seating when you order tickets. item.title: Friko Something Worth Waiting For : The Tour
item.description:
Thursday, September 10, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: White Oak Music Hall - Upstairs , 2915 N Main St,, Houston, TX

Doors: 7pm This show is ALL AGES All General Admission Tickets are Standing Room only IMPORTANT: WHITE OAK MUSIC HALL IS A CASHLESS VENUE. Please be sure to bring a debit or credit card. Friko has partnered with PLUS1 so that $1 from every ticket will go to Santa for a Day, a certified 501(c)(3) Public Charity who answers letters to Santa from kids in underserved communities. This two-pronged literacy component is vital to their mission: Volunteers helping, teaching youngsters the power of the pen? and then donors rewarding their efforts, by translating their wishes into reality, not only positively reinforces their nascent literacy skills, but it also helps participants author a different, more positive narrative about life, one sparked by optimism and the vision of a brighter future. santaforaday.org Doors: 7pm This show is ALL AGES All General Admission Tickets are Standing Room only IMPORTANT: WHITE OAK MUSIC HALL IS A CASHLESS VENUE. Please be sure to bring a debit or credit card. Friko has partnered with PLUS1 so that $1 from every ticket will go to Santa for a Day, a certified 501(c)(3) Public Charity who answers letters to Santa from kids in underserved communities. This two-pronged literacy component is vital to their mission: Volunteers helping, teaching youngsters the power of the pen? and then donors rewarding their efforts, by translating their wishes into reality, not only positively reinforces their nascent literacy skills, but it also helps participants author a different, more positive narrative about life, one sparked by optimism and the vision of a brighter future. santaforaday.org

item.title: The Music Man
item.description:
Tuesday, September 15, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: The hobby center, 800 Bagby St, Houston, TX 77002, United States, Houston, TX, 77002, United States

Seventy-six trombones. One unforgettable musical! Meredith Willson’s six-time Tony Award®-winning beloved musical comedy The Music Man marches into Houston with laughter, heart, and all the music you love. Following the fast-talking traveling salesman Harold Hill as he cons the people of River City, Iowa, into buying instruments and uniforms for a boys’ band that he vows to organize. Featuring songs such as “Seventy-Six Trombones,” “Trouble,” “’Till There Was You,” “Pick-a-Little,” and “Gary, Indiana,” The Music Man is a classic musical to be shared with every generation. CRITICAL ACCLAIM FOR THE MUSIC MAN – The Telegraph “A joyous, blissful Broadway event!” – Variety “Grand and Glorious!” “…totally enchanting abso-tootin’-lutely smashing revival. It will give you new life.” PERFORMANCE SCHEDULE Tuesday, Sept 15, 7:30 p.m. – H-E-B Community Night Wednesday, Sept 16, 7:30 p.m. Thursday, Sept 17, 7:30 p.m. Friday, Sept 18, 8 p.m. Saturday, Sept 19, 2 p.m. Saturday, Sept 19, 8 p.m. Sunday, Sept 20, 2 p.m. Sunday, Sept 20, 7:30 p.m. Tuesday, Sept 22, 7:30 p.m. Wednesday, Sept 23, 7:30 p.m. Thursday, Sept 24, 7:30 p.m. Friday, Sept 25, 8 p.m. Saturday, Sept 26, 2 p.m. — Open Captioned Saturday, Sept 26, 8 p.m. Sunday, Sept 27, 2 p.m. — ASL & Audio Described SUBSCRIBE NOW TICKETING INFO Season tickets are now available. 6-show packages start at just $34 per-show. Tickets will start at $46 and will be available beginning July 13 online or by calling TUTS Guest Services at 713-558-8887. Tickets will also available in person Monday through Friday from Noon to 6 p.m. at the TUTS Box Office, located in the Hobby Center lobby. Discounted group tickets are available for parties of 10 or more.Theatre Under The Stars has a variety of accessibility options for guests.Learn more about accessible performances and other services here. Rush tickets will be available for $35 at the box office for mezzanine and gallery seating starting 2 hours prior to curtain. Questions about parking, dining or other aspects of attending a TUTS show? We’ve got answers! RUN TIME 2 hours and 30 minutes including intermission Photos Photos by Marshall Meadows VIDEOS CREATIVE TEAM Matt Lenz Director Joshua Bergasse Choreographer Ann Beyersdorfer Set Designer Santo Loquasto Original Broadway and Tour Costume Designer Ken Billington Lighting Designer Lisa Renkel Video & Projection Designer Walter Trarbach Sound Designer Roxanne De Luna Wig & Makeup Designer Robin L. McGee Costume Coordinator Kristin Stowell Music Supervisor Thomas Fosnocht Music Director/Orchestrator ARC Casting Dan Sher/Big League Productions, Inc Producer BEHIND THE SCENES ELLIOTT ANDREWS as “Harold Hill” ELIZABETH D’AIUTO as “Marian Paroo” CHARLES ANTENEN as “Tommy Djilas” PATRICK BLASHILL as “Mayor Shinn” ADDIE JAYMES as “Amaryllis” DYLAN PATTERSON as “Winthrop Paroo” MICHAEL SANTORA as “Charlie Cowell” MADISON SHEWARD as “Zaneeta Shinn” SAVANNAH STEVENSON as “Mrs. Paroo” PAUL URRIOLA as “Marcellus Washburn” EMMANUELLE ZEESMAN as “Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” CHRISTIAN ANDREWS as “Conductor, Constable Locke, Ensemble, u/s Olin Britt” BRYCE BAYER as “Dance Ensemble Swing, Assistant Dance Captain” ANNA CHIN as “Dance Ensemble” DANNY CRUZ as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Tommy Djilas” KALISTA CURBELO as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Zaneeta Shinn” WHITNEY DANIELS as “Alma Hix, u/s Marian Paroo” ERIN DIEHL as “Dance Ensemble, Dance Captain” CRAIG FIRST as “Jacey Squires, u/s Marcellus Washburn, u/s Charlie Cowell” BRIAN DOOLITTLE GONZÁLEZ as “Ewart Dunlop, u/s Charlie Cowell, u/s Harold Hill” SOPHIE GORON as “Swing” ALEX ISHIDA as “Ethel Toffelmeir, u/s Marian Paroo” CAMERON JANSON as “Dance Ensemble” HANNAH ROSE KIDWELL as “Mrs. Squires, u/s Mrs. Paroo, u/s Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” KEVIN KUSKA as “Swing, u/s Marcellus Washburn, u/s Mayor Shinn” ROSE MESSENGER as “Maud Dunlop, u/s Mrs. Paroo, u/s Eulalie Mackecknie Shinn” JOSHUA PIERRE MOORE as “Olin Britt, u/s Mayor Shinn” KYLIE NOELLE PATTERSON as “Child Onstage Swing – Amaryllis/Winthrop” BRANDON RANALLI as “Dance Ensemble” AIDAN RAWLINSON as “Dance Ensemble, u/s Tommy Djilas” SHAE REYNOLDS as “Gracie Shinn, Dance Ensemble” BRAYDEN SCHILLING as “Traveling Salesperson, Ensemble, u/s Harold Hill, u/s Barbershop Quartet” MARIELLE UTAYDE as “Dance Ensemble Swing, u/s Zaneeta Shinn, u/s Gracie Shinn” MATTHEW WAUTIER-RODRIGUEZ as “Oliver Hix”

From Theatre Under The Stars
